pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh which explicitly downloads and saves the TIFF image to this exact path.
Verified via web search for USGS Landsat Collection 2 standard surface reflectance scaling factors.
Verified via web search for USGS Landsat Collection 2 standard surface reflectance scaling offsets.
Verified by downloading the source TIFF from opengeos and analyzing its shape with rasterio (4 bands).
Applying the floating-point radiometric formula (using scale 0.0000275 and offset -0.2) to the integer bands in SNAP yields float32. The export_result.sh checks for 'float' in DATA_TYPE.

privileged_info_summary: The required Landsat file is downloaded from a real Google Earth Engine Landsat TOA composite to /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif. The source TIFF was analyzed to have exactly 4 integer bands. The standard USGS Collection 2 radiometric calibration algorithm for surface reflectance indeed uses a scale factor of 0.0000275 and an offset of -0.2, which coerces the output bands to float32 upon calculation.
